question: What is the name of the ballet Valery Matveevich Panov created for the Istanbul Devlet Ballet in 1988?
report:
# Ballet Created by Valery Matveevich Panov for Istanbul Devlet Ballet (1988)

## TL;DR
Valery Matveevich Panov created the ballet **Cléopâtre** for the Istanbul Devlet Ballet in 1988.

## Background
Valery Matveevich Panov (12 March 1938 – 3 June 2025) was a prominent Soviet dancer and choreographer who performed with the Kirov Ballet from 1964 to 1972 before building an international career as a guest artist and director. He worked with companies in Berlin, Sweden, Norway and other European venues, staging works such as *Cinderella*, *The Rite of Spring* and *Hamlet* during the late 1970s and early 1980s.\[1\]

## Ballet for Istanbul Devlet Ballet
In 1988 Panov created the ballet *Cléopâtre* specifically for the Istanbul Devlet Ballet, adding a notable work to the Turkish company's repertoire.\[1\]

## References
[1] Wikipedia – Valery Panov – https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Valery_Panov

question: What was the title of the first recorded Cape slang (local Afrikaans dialect) hip-hop song in 1990 by Prophets of Da City?
report:
# Title: First Recorded Cape Slang Hip‑Hop Song by Prophets of Da City (1990)

## TL;DR
The first recorded Cape slang (local Afrikaans dialect) hip‑hop song released in 1990 by South Africa’s pioneering group Prophets of Da City is **"Dala Flat"**.[1][2]

## Answer
Prophets of Da City (often abbreviated POC) released their debut album *Our World* in 1990.  According to multiple sources, the track **"Dala Flat"** on that album is identified as the first recorded Cape slang rap‑song, using the local Afrikaans dialect in its lyrics.

### Evidence
- **Music In Africa** describes *Our World* (1990) and specifically notes that *"Dala Flat, the first Cape slang rap song"* appears on the album【c67539ab666f6849】.
- **Wikipedia** states that the album had *"the first recorded Cape slang (local Afrikaans dialect) hip‑hop song called 'Dala Flat'"*【56b31f79db257f70】.

Both sources agree on the song title and its significance as the earliest Cape‑slang hip‑hop recording.

## References
[1] Music In Africa – Prophets of Da City. https://www.musicinafrica.net/directory/prophets-da-city
[2] Wikipedia – Prophets of Da City. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Prophets_of_Da_City

question: On June 24, 2018, how many more votes did the winning candidate get in total than Muharrem İnce?
report:
# Vote difference between the winning candidate and Muharrem İnce in the June 24, 2018 Turkish presidential election

## TL;DR
The winning candidate, President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan, received **10,990,502 more votes** than challenger Muharrem İnce.

## Vote totals
- **Recep Tayyip Erdoğan** – 26,330,823 votes【1】.
- **Muharrem İnce** – 15,340,321 votes【2】.

## Difference in votes
The margin between Erdoğan and İnce is:

26,330,823 − 15,340,321 = **10,990,502 votes**【2】.

## References
[1] AA – "Erdogan won polls with 52.59 pct votes: Election body" – https://www.aa.com.tr/en/politics/erdogan-won-polls-with-5259-pct-votes-election-body/1194888
[2] Xinhua – "Turkey announces final results of June 24 general elections" – http://www.xinhuanet.com/english/2018-07/04/c_137301742.htm

question: Who is the municipality of Donmatías, Antioquia, Colombia, named after?
report:
# Who is the municipality of Donmatías, Antioquia, Colombia named after?

## TL;DR
Donmatías received its name in 1787 in honor of **Don Matías Jaramillo**, a local miner who owned a prosperous mining establishment in the area.

## Naming origin
The municipality’s current name was adopted in 1787 to honor **Don Matías Jaramillo**. Historical records state that he was a man who owned a rich mining settlement in the territory that later became the town [[1]].

## When the name was chosen
The official naming occurred in the year **1787** when the settlement was renamed from its earlier names (Azuero, San Antonio del Infante) to Donmatías in recognition of Jaramillo’s influence [[1]].

## What Don Matías Jaramillo was known for
Don Matías Jaramillo is described as a miner with a **prosperous mining establishment** in the region. His mining activities were significant enough to lend his name to the municipality [[2]].

## References
[1] English Wikipedia – "Don Matías" – https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Don_Mat%C3%ADas
[2] Spanish Wikipedia – "Donmatías" – https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Donmat%C3%ADas

question: Who is known to be the first female museum curator in Spain's National Archaeological Museum (Madrid)?
report:
# First Female Museum Curator at Spain's National Archaeological Museum (Madrid)

## TL;DR
María del Pilar Fernández Vega (also known as Pilar Fernández) became the first female museum curator in Spain when she transferred to the National Archaeological Museum (MAN) in Madrid in **1928**, heading its Pre‑Columbian collection. [1][2]

## Who was the first female curator?
The pioneer was **María del Pilar Fernández Vega** (1895‑1973), a Spanish scholar and museum professional. Contemporary records describe her as the first woman to hold a curatorial post in any Spanish museum. [1][2]

## Year of appointment
Both primary sources agree that she obtained the curatorial position in **1928** after requesting a transfer from the National Museum of Anthropology. [1][2]

## Role within the museum
At the National Archaeological Museum she was appointed **head of the Pre‑Columbian section**, responsible for the museum’s collections of American artefacts. [1]

## Caveats and open questions
- The sources focus on her appointment and role; details about her later career (e.g., later directorship of the Museum of America) are outside the scope of this answer.
- No contradictory accounts were found; both references corroborate the same facts.

## References
[1] Wikipedia – María del Pilar Fernández Vega. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mar%C3%ADa_del_Pilar_Fern%C3%A1ndez_Vega
[2] Dialnet – *Pioneras de la museología en España*. https://dialnet.unirioja.es/servlet/articulo?codigo=8977041

question: On what month, day, and year did Bernie Sanders sponsor the Cancer Registries Amendment Act?
report:
# Sponsorship Date of the Cancer Registries Amendment Act

## TL;DR
Representative Bernie Sanders sponsored the Cancer Registries Amendment Act (H.R. 4206) on **February 7, 1992**.

## Sponsorship Details
The bill, officially designated **H.R. 4206** in the 102nd Congress, was introduced in the House of Representatives on **February 7, 1992** by Rep. Bernie Sanders of Vermont. This date marks the formal sponsorship of the Cancer Registries Amendment Act.

- Sponsor: Rep. Bernie Sanders (I‑VT) [1]
- Introduction date: February 7, 1992 [1]
- Confirmation from Sanders Institute narrative stating the sponsorship occurred in February 1992 [2]

## References
[1] GovTrack – "Cancer Registries Amendment Act (1992 – H.R. 4206)" – https://www.govtrack.us/congress/bills/102/hr4206
[2] Sanders Institute – "Rep. Bernie Sanders Sponsors Cancer Registries Amendment Act (HR 4206)" – https://sandersinstitute.org/event/rep-bernie-sanders-sponsors-cancer-registries-amendment-act-hr-4206

question: What is the surname of the individual who won the Faraday Lectureship Prize, previously known simply as the Faraday Lectureship, in 1914?
report:
# Surname of the 1914 Faraday Lectureship Prize Winner

## TL;DR
The Faraday Lectureship Prize awarded in 1914 was won by Hermann Emil **Fischer**; the surname is **Fischer**.

## Winner Details
The Faraday Lectureship Prize, previously known simply as the Faraday Lectureship, listed **Hermann Emil Fischer** as its 1914 recipient【1】. Fischer was a renowned German chemist and Nobel laureate, and his surname is the answer to the query.

## References
[1] Faraday Lectureship Prize – Wikipedia, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Faraday_Lectureship_Prize
